{{Quote|Can you think of a better prison than one without walls? What does your species use? Cages, I suppose.|translated=1|The Imprisoned One|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
'''Mind prisons''' were technological devices of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] that were created by the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] [[Species/Legends|species]] as a means of [[Prison/Legends|incarcerating]] criminals and other beings they considered unsavory. However, these prisons were unusual in that an individual's physical body was not caged, but rather the mind. In part due to [[Rakatan plague|losing their connection]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], the Infinite Empire collapsed in [[25,200 BBY]], and the remaining Rakata fled their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]], where they splintered into the various tribes of the [[Rakatan Archipelago]]. A number of tribes took with them the technology of their ancestors, including mind prisons, while other devices became forgotten in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

The reality of mind traps were lost in the obscurity of history until [[3956 BBY]], when the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] was commissioned to transport a mind trap—known to him as a mysterious box—from [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. In the process, he learned of the existence of mind traps and a small part of their history.At a later date, [[Ruthic|Elder Ruthic]], a descendant of the ancient Rakata, gained the leadership position in the [[Tulpaa tribe]], located in the Rakatan Archipelago, by the use of mind prisons. Being unique due to her growing connection to the Force, Ruthic was able to activate the ancient mind prisons of her people and banish both the tribe's former leaders and her enemies.

==Function==
{{Quote|My own people put our criminals in little cages like this. Well… our minds, anyway.|translated=1|The Imprisoned One|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Mind prisons were devices invented by [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Sentience/Legends|individuals]] of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]], sometime before [[25,200 BBY]],<ref name="JvS" /> as a means of [[Prison/Legends|incarcerating]] criminals of their own [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="KotOR" /> and other beings they considered unacceptable, such as [[Sharu/Legends|Sharu]] malefactors and [[Kwa/Legends|Kwa]] super-[[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].<ref name="TUR" /> However, the Rakata did not cage their enemies' physical bodies, but rather their minds. The [[Time/Legends|length]] of detention varied; individuals considered to have committed terrible crimes expected to be confined for eternity with no means of escape.<ref name="KotOR" />

After the fall of the Infinite Empire, mind prisons became scattered around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]],<ref name="TUR" /><ref name="KotOR" /> most notably to the [[Planet/Legends|planets]] of the [[Rakatan Archipelago]], [[Makatak]] and [[Tulpaa]]. However, their function essentially remained the same: to constrain undesirable persons<ref name="TUR" /> or as a storage area for living beings.<ref name="CSWEMotta" />

==Properties==
{{Quote|Just do not open it… that is all I can say. It represents no danger to you so long as it remains closed.|translated=1|Lurze Kesh|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
[[File:Rakprison.jpg|thumb|Rakatan Prison|220px|left|The "inside" of a Rakatan prison]]
The outward appearance of a mind prison resembled an ordinary cargo crate,<ref name="CSWE" /> although there was at least one example that was vaguely pyramidal in shape and had small engravings of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] visages running along its vertical faces.<ref name="KotOR" /> The interior of the prison was a "room" composed of an infinite expanse of white nothingness.<ref name="TUR" /> However, the aforementioned pyramidal prison was known to have contained a series of rune-covered pillars and a bed.<ref name="KotOR" />

The prison box itself was not considered hazardous,<ref name="KotOR" /> unless improperly opened. In that instance, the individual would find their mind under the control of the prison trap and drawn inside. Often, the victim would be confronted by a long-dead Rakata whose only chance for escape was to take over the victim's body. The Imprisoned One asked the newcomer a series of riddles and if they were answered incorrectly, the Rakata would inhabit the body and attempt an escape.<ref name="CSWE" />

The fall of the Infinite Empire caused many Rakatan tribes to scatter to various worlds, bringing their ancient technology with them. Two tribes of the Rakatan Archipelago, the [[Makatak tribe|Makatak]] and [[Tulpaa tribe]]s, possessed dozens of mind prisons that could only be opened by use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="TUR" /> Other prisons, such as the [[The Imprisoned One's mind prison|one owned]] by [[Motta]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], were believed to have lost their protections, thereby affording the prisoners a means of exiting.<ref name="KotOR" />

==History==
{{Quote|Are my people still about? If they were, I can't see that they'd let my prison loose so easily.|translated=1|The Imprisoned One|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Before the decline of the Infinite Empire in 25,200 BBY,<ref name="JvS" /> the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Rakata tribes invented many Force-based technological devices.<ref name="TUR" /> The mind prison was developed as a form of imprisonment that was considered to be both superior and secure.<ref name="KotOR" /> The fall of the Empire led to the destabilization of all planets under Rakatan control<ref name="TUR" /> with much of their technology being destroyed<ref name="KotOR" /> or scattered. Furthermore, the few remaining tribes on the Rakatan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]], fled the planet to settle on other worlds—forming the Rakatan Archipelago—taking their technology, including mind prisons, with them.<ref name="TUR" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
[[File:Motta1.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Motta the Hutt, an owner of a mind prison]]
In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="KotORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] visited [[Dreshdae]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], where he was approached by [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Lurze Kesh]], a procurer of unusual goods. Kesh had recognized Revan's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', as a former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] vessel, and assuming that Revan was a smuggler, Kesh offered Revan a job: delivering a mysterious box to Motta the Hutt in [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Revan accepted the commission, with Kesh warning the Jedi to not open the box. Revan disregarded the Rodian's advice, and became trapped inside the coffer. Upon entering, Revan was greeted by an [[The Imprisoned One|ancient Rakatan being]] who informed him that his mind was now trapped inside a mind prison. However, the antediluvian had discovered a means of escape—theorizing that this was only possible due to the prison having lost its protections—and he offered to show Revan the exit if the Jedi was the victor in a contest of riddles. Revan bested the Rakata and went on to deliver the mind prison to Motta.<ref name="KotOR" />
{{Endgame}}

Some time later,<ref name="Date">The ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' campaign guide doesn't give an exact date of when [[Ruthic]] lived, but the guide does say that the Rakata tribes are most likely to be encountered during the Old Republic era. Furthermore, it states that Ruthic's tribe, the [[Tulpaa tribe|Tulpaa]], is building a small-scale version of the [[Star Forge]] that was destroyed at the end of the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Therefore, it is assumed that Elder Ruthic lived in the Old Republic era after the time of Revan.</ref> [[Ruthic|Elder Ruthic]], a [[Gender/Legends|female]] Rakata priest, rose to the leadership position of the Tulpaa tribe by banishing the tribe's former leaders, and other enemies, into a mind trap. Shortly before the collapse of the Infinite Empire, the Rakata had [[Rakatan plague|lost their connection]] to the Force. However, unlike the preceding generations, Ruthic was unique in that she had an emerging bond with the Force, enabling her to make use of the technology of her ancestors. Furthermore, Ruthic gained a wise counselor from her use of mind prisons. Upon banishing opponents to a [[Tulpaa mind prison|mind prison]], a pure-[[Blood/Legends|blooded]] [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] named [[Raspir]] was released. Having once served [[Sith Empire (Pre-Republic)|Sith]] [[King/Legends|King]] [[Adas]] as a [[Sith sorcerer|court magician]], Raspir's proficiency with [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] [[Magic/Legends|magic]] proved beneficial to the tribe leader.<ref name="TUR" />

==Behind the scenes==
The mind prison first appeared in [[BioWare]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]], released in [[2003]], as a "mysterious box." The object is part of an optional courier mission given by Lurze Kesh; however, in order to unlock the courier mission, the player must gain access to the secret compartment within the ''Ebon Hawk''. Furthermore, the choice to open the mysterious box is optional as well. Completion of either of the aforementioned missions does not affect the player's [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light-side]] or dark-side alignment.<ref name="KotOR" />

With the publication of ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[2008]], the mind prison received its own entry that further described the object's properties. Moreover, the entry for Motta the Hutt seemed to indicate that the mysterious box seen in ''Knights of the Old Republic'' was only one example of a mind prison.<ref name="CSWEMotta" />

The release of ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'', a [[Wizards of the Coast]] supplement to the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'', in [[2010]] established the existence of many mind prisons, in addition to confirming that the mind prisons had been created by the ancient Rakata before their Infinite Empire collapsed.<ref name="JvS" />

In his endnotes for ''The Unknown Regions'', co-author [[Daniel Wallace]] stated that Raspir, King Adas's former court magician, was approximately 28,000 years old, which suggested that mind prisons were, at the very least, of that age. It also suggested that mind prisons were used by King Adas and his Sith Empire. However, as these two points had not been explicitly confirmed, they were not included in the [[Star Wars Legends|official]] history of mind prisons.<ref name="Endnotes">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/04/endnotes-for-star-wars-unknown-regions.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for ''The Unknown Regions''}}</ref>
